Items.Title: 测试 JavaScript 代码的 13 种最佳工具 | Best 13 Tools To Test JavaScript Code
Items.Description:
单元和功能测试是验证 JavaScript 代码质量的主要方法。 但是，有许多工具也可以在代码执行之前或期间检查代码，以测试其质量和对编码标准的遵守情况。 每个工具都有其独特的特性和优势，有助于其测试能力，您可以使用最适合您执行JavaScript 测试需求的工具。

考虑到 JavaScript 工具的绝对数量，选择适合您的工具可能具有挑战性。 但是，一种简化流程的简单方法是在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码。 是的，您可以直接在浏览器中测试您的 JavaScript 代码。 为此，您可以使用多种工具。

本文列出了 13 种最佳工具，可帮助您在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码。

为什么要测试你的 JavaScript 代码？

截至目前，大多数网站都使用 JavaScript 作为其客户端编程语言。 通过 HTML 和图形元素集成、Silverlight 或 Flash 等高级插件以及文档对象模型 (DOM)，JavaScript 在动态 Web 开发中保持了其至高无上的地位。

由于敏捷开发和严格集成已成为软件开发过程中不可或缺的一部分，因此对开发的组件进行持续测试变得至关重要。 由于它广泛用于前端和后端组件，因此在初始软件开发周期中测试您的 JavaScript 代码非常重要。

假设您正在处理一个 JavaScript 代码块。 如果不在本地系统上进行任何单独安装，只在浏览器中测试它不是更好吗？ 但这在很大程度上取决于您选择用于测试代码的工具或框架。

由于 JavaScript 是一种动态语言（弱类型），因此很难调试或测试 JavaScript 代码。 因此，您需要巧妙地选择众多工具、框架和技术中的一种来在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码。

在功能最强大的在线Android 模拟器上跨所有旧版和最新移动操作系统测试您的本机、混合和 Web 应用程序。

如何在浏览器中启用 JavaScript？

由于 JavaScript 是一种运行在访问者浏览器上的脚本语言，因此现在几乎每个浏览器都必须提供对 JavaScript 页面的支持。 通常，浏览器会预先启用 JavaScript 的支持。 但是，如果不是这种情况，您可以手动启用它。 在这篇关于如何在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码的博客中，我们将继续以 Google Chrome 作为主要示例：

  1. 在您的 Google Chrome 窗口中，导航至三点菜单并转到设置。

  2. 从左侧边栏中选择隐私和安全，然后选择站点设置。

  1. 选择单选按钮： < > Sites can use JavaScript 。

你完成了！

现在，您可以通过两种方式在 Chrome 浏览器中查看和调试网页的 JavaScript 代码：

  1. 当您的 JavaScript 代码包含在 HTML 文件中时，同时按下Ctrl + U键盘按钮。

或者

  1. 当你有一个单独的.js 文件时，右键单击网页的任何区域，然后从菜单中选择检查元素选项。

假设您要查看或调试 Google 着陆页的 JavaScript。 它看起来像这样。

您当然可以使用此方法调试您的 JavaScript 日志。 尽管如此，您仍需要使用本指南中提到的工具在浏览器中对您的 JavaScript 代码执行正确的端到端测试，而不依赖于Jest 、 Mocha等框架。

在开始使用这些工具之前，让我们看看您可以执行哪些测试。

在本教程中，了解什么是回归测试、它的重要性、类型以及如何执行它。

您可以执行什么类型的测试？

软件测试范围很广，但您必须为您的 JavaScript 代码运行这三个测试。

单元测试：该测试遵循分而治之的原则。 由于大多数程序都涉及各种功能、类和模块之间的交互，因此将代码划分为隔离和独立的单元很容易。 您还可以将它们拆分为服务器端单元和客户端单元。

集成测试：这种类型的测试是对单元测试的补充。 一旦所有单元/模块放在一起，功能可能会失败，即使所有模块都成功通过了单元测试用例。 集成测试用于识别和消除此类实例。

端到端 (E2E) 测试：单独测试单元和模块后，下一步就是进行端到端测试。 这些测试会自动执行用户操作，例如鼠标点击和输入。 但是，E2E 测试执行起来比较棘手。 通常，他们需要专门的自动化测试框架。

在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码的 13 个最佳工具

既然我们已经讨论了测试 JavaScript 代码的重要性以及在浏览器中执行这些测试的好处，现在是探索如何使用一些最佳工具和框架在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码的时候了。

在浏览器中测试 JavaScript 代码的前 13 名最流行的工具是 –

  1. 代码笔

  2. 代码沙箱

  3. 该死的代码

  4. 贾斌

  5. 谷歌浏览器 + VSCode

  6. 活织

  7. 艾迪酮

  8. 堆栈比尔茨

  9. 复制

通过 LambdaTest 测试您的 JavaScript 代码可确保正常运行，并允许您分析脚本在各种设备、浏览器和操作系统上的行为。 访问可扩展的云基础架构，以测试运行最新和旧版浏览器的 Windows、macOS、Android 和 iOS 版本的 JavaScript。

  • 在不同的浏览器、设备和操作系统上捕获自动屏幕截图。

  • 使用 LambdaTest，您可以通过我们的真实设备功能生成器轻松配置您的自动化框架测试。

    • 现在，在最强大的云基础设施上执行浏览器自动化测试。 利用 LambdaTest 自动化测试平台在云上获得更快、可靠和可扩展的体验。

    代码笔

    它就像一个提供在线学习环境的社交媒体社区。 它在十年前的 2012 年推出。截至今天，它拥有超过 339,000 名注册用户。 如果你想测试你的前端技能，你可以参加 codepen 上的月度挑战。 它每个月都有一个新主题，每周都有一组新的挑战。 付费订阅计划提供更多 CodePen 功能，例如协作模式、资产托管、模板和自定义域。

    您还可以使用允许您使用不同库（如 React）的外部资源。 在 CodePen 中，您的个人资料就像您的前端开发组合。 你可以在那里展示你所有最好的作品。

    它有许多来自不同 HTML 和 CSS 开发人员的笔，展示他们的工作并帮助其他人向他们学习。 有了所有这些提到的功能，CodePen 允许您轻松地在浏览器中测试您的 JavaScript 代码。 您也可以选择它作为您的在线前端开发伴侣。

    代码沙箱

    它是一个在线代码编辑器，用于在浏览器中快速编写和测试 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 代码。 最好的部分：它不需要任何注册。 它还带有一个非常用户友好的用户界面。 它支持许多流行的框架，如 React、Next、Vue、Angular 和 Vanilla JS。 用户在编写代码时在输出屏幕上获得即时更新。

    您还可以从 GitHub 导入代码或从您的计算机粘贴代码以获取实时更新和实时协作。 个人使用免费。 其中一些是。

    • 实时协作——在 CodeSandbox 上工作时，您可以打开一个实时会话，该会话将生成一个唯一的 URL，您可以让其他人加入您的沙箱并进行协作，例如 Google 文档。

    • 分叉从 GitHub 导入的沙箱时，您可以进行更改并创建 PR 或在完成后提交更改。

    • 当您想创建沙箱的生产版本时，可以单击“使用 Vercel 部署”。 这将部署沙箱、安装 node_modules 并在新 URL 下启动它。 然后它返回此 URL，您可以将其用于您喜欢的任何域。

    需要适用于 Windows 的 Safari的出色解决方案？ 忘掉仿真器或模拟器吧——使用真正的在线浏览器。 免费试用 LambdaTest。

    该死的代码

    您可以使用它与您的朋友协作编码，而无需在您的计算机上下载任何内容。 它是一个类似于 CodeSandbox 的在线平台，您可以在其中测试 JavaScript 代码。 它是免费的，而且比大多数浏览器内的 IDE 更高效。 它还提供 Web 开发课程，如全栈学习路径。

    它还提供对 Nextjs 和 Reactjs 等流行框架的访问。 然而，Codedamn 游乐场最近才发布，并没有一个庞大的社区。

    它具有非常用户友好的界面和快速的响应时间。 它还兼容流行的 JavaScript 框架，如 Vue、React、Nextjs 等。它共有 6 种不同的布局可供选择，并提供了十多个框架。

    除了语法突出显示之外，JSFiddle 还具有简洁的“整理”功能，可以适当地缩进和分隔代码。 此外，您可以使用 JSLint 检查 JavaScript 语法。

    当您编辑小提琴（代码片段）时，您可以通过单击“更新”按钮创建新版本或使用“分叉”按钮分叉代码。 拥有更新按钮的好处是它会为小提琴创建一个带有版本号的新 URL。 这个 URL 和版本号可以很容易地从最近的小提琴回到以前的版本，以防你需要参考最后的代码。

    本Cypress 自动化测试教程将帮助您了解 Cypress 自动化的优势，以及如何安装 Cypress 并在线对多种浏览器和操作系统执行 Cypress 自动化测试。

    Unit and functional testing are the prime ways of verifying the JavaScript code quality. However, a host of tools are available that can also check code before or during its execution in order to test its quality and adherence to coding standards. With each tool having its unique features and advantages contributing to its testing capabilities, you can use the tool that best suits your need for performing JavaScript testing.

    Considering the sheer number of JavaScript tools, choosing one that is right for you can be challenging. But an easy way to smoothen the process is to test JavaScript code in a browser. Yes, you can test your JavaScript code directly in a browser. To do this, you can use a variety of tools.

    This article lists the best 13 tools to help you test JavaScript code in browsers.

    Why test your JavaScript code?

    As of now, the majority of websites use JavaScript as their client-side programming language. With HTML and graphical elements integration, advanced plugins like Silverlight or Flash, and the Document Object Model (DOM), JavaScript has maintained its supremacy in dynamic web development.

    A recent survey by Statista shows that JavaScript will be the most popular programming language in 2022, with over 65% stating they use JavaScript and less than 55% using HTML/CSS.

    Since agile development and rigorous integration have become a non-negotiable part of the software development process, continuous testing of the developed components has become crucial. The same goes for JavaScript. With it being extensively used in front-end and back-end components, it’s important to test your JavaScript code during the initial software development cycles.

    Popular agile development methods like Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) and Test-Driven Development (TDD) work the same way, i.e., testing components as a fundamental building block.

    Consider yourself working on a JavaScript code block. Wouldn’t it be better just to test it in a browser, without any separate installation on your local system? But that too dramatically depends on the tool or framework you choose for testing the code.

    As JavaScript is a dynamic language (weakly typed), it becomes difficult to debug or test JavaScript code. Hence, you need to smartly choose one of the many tools, frameworks, and techniques to test JavaScript code in a browser.

    Test your native, hybrid, and web apps across all legacy and latest mobile operating systems on the most powerful Android emulator online.

    How to enable JavaScript in a browser?

    As JavaScript is a scripting language that runs on a visitor’s browsers, almost every browser nowadays has to provide support for JavaScript pages. Typically, browsers come with the support of JavaScript pre-enabled. However, you can enable it manually if that’s not the case. In this blog on how to test JavaScript code in browsers, we will continue with Google Chrome as our primary example:

    1. On your Google Chrome window, navigate to the three dots menu and go to Settings.

    2. From the left sidebar, select Privacy and security, then Site settings.

    1. Select the radio button that says: < > Sites can use JavaScript.

    And you are done!

    Now, you can view and debug JavaScript code for a webpage in your Chrome browser in two ways:

    1. When your JavaScript code is wrapped within the HTML file, press the Ctrl + U keyboard buttons simultaneously.

    OR

    1. When you have a separate .js file, right-click on any area of the web page and select the Inspect element option from the menu.

    Let’s say you want to view or debug JavaScript for the Google landing page. It looks something like this.

    You can surely debug your JavaScript logs with this method. Still, you need to perform proper end to end testing of your JavaScript code in the browser with the tools mentioned in this guide without relying on frameworks like Jest, Mocha, etc.

    Before getting started with the tools, let’s see what tests you can perform.

    In this tutorial, learn what is Regression testing, its importance, types, and how to perform it.

    What type of tests can you perform?

    Software testing is vast, but you must run these three tests for your JavaScript code.

    Unit testing: This test works on the divide and conquers principle. Unit testing checks the performance of an isolated unit, i.e., a function or a class. As most programs involve interaction between various functions, classes, and modules, dividing a code into isolated and independent units is easy. You can also split these into server-side units and client-side units.

    Integration testing: This type of testing acts as a complement to unit testing. Integration testing mainly focuses on how different components or units interact during the test execution. The functionality may fail once all the units/modules are put together, even though all the modules passed unit test cases successfully. Integration testing is used to identify and eradicate such instances.

    End to end (E2E) testing: Once you’ve tested units and modules individually, the next step is to conduct an end-to-end test. E2E tests allow you to run a full functionality experience that includes both the front-end and back-end components. These tests automate user actions like mouse clicks and inputs. However, E2E tests are trickier to execute. Generally, they require dedicated automated testing frameworks.

    步骤 4为名为pg_age的数据库集群启动数据库服务器

    cd postgres/postgresql-11.18 bin/pg_ctl -D pg_age -l logfile start

    步骤 5转到目录 age-viewer

    第 6 步在本地系统上设置 AGE Viewer 开发服务器。

     npm run setup npm run start

    这会将您带到在本地主机上运行的 AGE 查看器

    步骤 7使用要连接的数据库配置 Viewer。 在此处提供详细信息：

     Connect URL : 127.0.0.1 Connect Port: 5432 Database Name: practicedb Username: your_username_on_linux Password: your_system_password

    开始查询数据库

    确保数据库服务器正在运行。 示例查询：

    创建图表

    SELECT create_graph('country');

    使用标签 City 和 property name 创建顶点

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ CREATE (a: City {name: "A"}), (b: City {name : "B"}), (c : City {name : "C"}), (d: City {name : "D"}), (e : City {name : "E"}) $$) AS (result agtype);

    查看所有城市(verices)

     SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH (u : City) RETURN u $$) AS (result agtype);

    它显示了创建的 5 个顶点

    在节点之间创建边作为

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH (a: City {name: "A"}), (b: City {name : "B"}) CREATE (a)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 2}]->(b) $$) AS (result agtype); SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH (b: City {name: "B"}), (c: City {name : "C"}) CREATE (b)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 1}]->(c) $$) AS (result agtype); SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH (a: City {name: "A"}), (d: City {name : "D"}) CREATE (a)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 4}]->(d) $$) AS (result agtype); SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH (d: City {name: "D"}), (e: City {name : "E"}) CREATE (d)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 3}]->(e) $$) AS (result agtype);

    查看图表

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$ MATCH(u : City)-[e : CONNECTED]->(v : City) RETURN u, e, v $$) AS (u agtype, e agtype, v agtype);

    在这里尝试一些有趣的事情你会注意到从 A 到 C 和 A 到 D 有一个可变长度的边，即； 参考：

    Zainab-Saad

    Posted on Mar 15

    Apache AGE Viewer is a web user interface for the visualization of graphs created in AGE - A multi-model database that supports graph database on top of the already existing relational database while also supporting JSON and key-value data.

    AGE Viewer has the following prospects:

    • Provides data visualization
    • Supports complex graph queries
    • Helps users discover the relationships among strongly connected entities in the graph

    Prerequisites

    You should have the following installed on your system.

    • git
    • postgreSQL
    • Apache AGE

    If you are on linux, follow the guide on How to install postgreSQL and Apache AGE from source code

    Installing AGE Viewer

    Step 1 Go to the directory postgres_age
    cd postgres_age

    Step 2 Install node.js and npm. It is recommened to install using node version manager. nvm manages different versions of node on your system and thus prevents any clashes between the different projects using different node version. Install nvm following the guide here
    You can check if nvm is installed correctly using nvm --version
    Check if the specified version of node is installed using node --version

    Step 3 Clone the AGE Viewer GitHub repository
    git clone https://github.com/apache/age-viewer.git

    Step 4 Start the database server for the database cluster named pg_age

    cd postgres/postgresql-11.18
bin/pg_ctl -D pg_age -l logfile start

    Step 5 Go to directory age-viewer

    Step 6 Setup AGE Viewer development server on local system.

    npm run setup
npm run start

    This takes you to AGE Viewer running on localhost

    Step 7 Configure Viewer with the database you want to connect. Provide the details as here:

    Connect URL : 127.0.0.1
Connect Port: 5432
Database Name: practicedb
Username: your_username_on_linux
Password: your_system_password

    Start querying the database

    Make sure you have the database server running. Example queries:

    Create graph

    SELECT create_graph('country');

    Create vertices with the label City and property name

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
CREATE (a: City {name: "A"}),
(b: City {name : "B"}),
(c : City {name : "C"}),
(d: City {name : "D"}),
(e : City {name : "E"})
$$) AS (result agtype);

    View all the cities(verices)

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH (u : City)
RETURN u
$$) AS (result agtype);

    It shows the 5 vertices created as

    Create edges between nodes as

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH (a: City {name: "A"}), (b: City {name : "B"})
CREATE (a)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 2}]->(b)
$$) AS (result agtype);

SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH (b: City {name: "B"}), (c: City {name : "C"})
CREATE (b)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 1}]->(c)
$$) AS (result agtype);

SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH (a: City {name: "A"}), (d: City {name : "D"})
CREATE (a)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 4}]->(d)
$$) AS (result agtype);

SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH (d: City {name: "D"}), (e: City {name : "E"})
CREATE (d)-[e1: CONNECTED {time : 3}]->(e)
$$) AS (result agtype);

    View the graph

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH(u : City)-[e : CONNECTED]->(v : City)
RETURN u, e, v
$$) AS (u agtype, e agtype, v agtype);

    Try something interesting here You can notice that there is a variable length edge from A to C and A to D i.e; A is indirectly connected to C and E

    SELECT * FROM cypher('country', $$
MATCH(a : City {name : "A"})-[e : CONNECTED* ]->(x : City)
RETURN a, e, x
$$) AS (a agtype, e agtype, x agtype);

Items.Description:
杂志：

在本节中，我应该借助 HTML 和 CSS 创建一个 Cafe Menu。 我必须在菜单中添加两个部分，一个是咖啡和甜点。

我学会了如何在<script>标签的帮助下在 HTML 本身中添加 CSS 代码。 但它变得难以管理，而是使用带有 .css 扩展名的单独文件并将其与<link>标记链接。

代码片段：

 <link href="styles.css" rel="stylesheet"/>

这里的rel定义了链接资源和文档 HTML 之间的关系。

背景：
我们可以使用background-color等属性来改变颜色， background-image来改变背景图像等等。

文本：
利润：
展示：
为了在微观层面上正确地改变样式，我们可以使用<div>并创建新的分区只是为了在它们上面添加新的样式属性。 此外，我们可以将class属性设置为样式并访问特定内容。

填充：
一些构成属性是padding-left 、 padding-right 、 padding-top 、 padding-bottom 。

字体：
还有一些。

在创建 Cafe Menu 时，我没有遇到任何挑战或错误。 所以，今天的会议非常顺利。

我还在我的GitHub上发布了这些笔记。

生产率：

生产力得分：7
享受分数：7

与昨天相比，我今天的工作效率更高，但仍有很大的改进空间。 我喜欢应对挑战和调试，因为它们让我可以更深入地研究。 虽然我对最终网页感到满意，但我发现它太简单了。

外部链接：

我电脑上的墙纸： Marko
生产力得分： Ruby Granger

我上课时听了一些音乐。



原文:

Journal:

In this section, I'm supposed to create a Cafe Menu with the help of HTML and CSS. I have to add two sections in the menu one for Coffee and Desserts.

I learnt how to add CSS codes inside the HTML itself with the help of <script> tag. But it gets difficult to manage, rather having separate file with .css extension and linking that with <link> tag.

Code Snipet:

<link href="styles.css" rel="stylesheet"/>

Here rel defines the relationship between linked resource and the document HTML.

Background:
background property sets the background. We can constiuent properties like background-color to change the color, background-image to change the backgorund image and many more.

Text:
text-align to set up the alignment of content.

Margin:
margin-left, margin-right for setting the margin in left and right.

Display:
display property for setting the element as block, inline and some more.

In order to properly change the styling at a micro level, we can use <div> and create new division just to add new styling properties on them. Also, we can class attribute to style and access specific contents.

Padding:
padding property to set padding on 4 sides of an element. Some constituent properties are padding-left, padding-right, padding-top, padding-bottom.

Font:
font-family, font-size these are properties to change the styling of fonts in an element. There are some more.

While creating the Cafe Menu, I didn't face any challenges or errors. So, it was a very smooth session today.

I also have published the notes on my GitHub.

Productivity:

Productivity Score: 7
Enjoyment Score: 7

Compared to yesterday, I had a more productive day today, but there is still a lot of room for improvement. I enjoy tackling challenges and debugging because they allow me to delve deeper. While I was satisfied with the final webpage, I found it to be too easy.

Items.Title: 每个人都应该知道的 5 大 GraphQL 缺点 | Top 5 GraphQL Disadvantages Everyone Should Know
Items.Description:
它拥有许多优势，例如提供更有效的数据检索方式以及使开发人员能够以声明方式指定他们需要的数据。 然而，与任何技术一样，GraphQL 也有开发人员应该注意的缺点。 在本文中，我们将研究 GraphQL 的前 5 大缺点，并讨论它们如何影响您的开发过程。

验证

在 GQL 中，我们有一个模式，我们在其中描述请求（查询、变更、类型）。 它与 REST 有什么不同？ 毕竟，事实上，API端点变化的应用是不存在的。 但并非一切都是这样。 在最简单的示例中，您有应用端点，其中一些端点可供未授权用户使用，其余部分可供授权用户使用，并且这两部分不相交。 好的，显然，所有 API 端点都可用于经过身份验证的客户端。 在 GQL 上模拟它是行不通的。 例如，您有一个方案，其中大多数请求对未经授权的用户不可用 - 直到他调用它们，才有办法知道它。 这就是为什么我们从方案中不清楚如何使用 API。

突变堆

如果查询的构建方式使得我们可以清楚地了解我们可以从哪个实体开始编写查询以及最终我们实际上可以获得什么，那么 Mutations 就有问题了。 我们打开 GQL 模式文档，看到大量可用于与服务通信的操作......

而且我需要多说一点，大多数情况下，开发人员为 Mutations 选择了非常糟糕的命名，并且很难在文档中找到连接到同一实体的所有操作，例如 GitHub GQL API。

文件

只要我们通过 GQL 交换数据，一切都很好。 但是迟早，您将需要下载文件。 是的，我们有一个multipart/form-data ，我们可以在其中将流文件附加到请求并让服务器支持它。 恭喜，这违反了 GQL 的基本方法之一 - 传输不可知论。 错误处理

该错误可能是以下两种类型之一：

  • 错误- 业务逻辑错误，和
  • 异常——这意味着你对一些错误的数据、实体等做了一些错误的操作。

在异常的情况下，我们有顶级异常的概念，当我们被直接告知：“请求因某某原因而失败，因为......”。

对于业务逻辑错误，情况有所不同：您必须自己描述不同情况下每个突变的错误。 但它们将采用何种格式，将携带何种信息——这些都没有标准化。

缓存

当我们使用 CRUD 时，我们有一个可用于请求的 HTTP 方法列表（GET、POST 等）。

拒绝数据幂等性剥夺了我们非常高的准确性——当我们以粗略的形式执行请求时，我们可以返回一个Cache头并指示我们应该缓存一些数据一段时间，因此浏览器将其放入缓存并选择为我们准备好数据。 显然，这加快了服务器在客户端-服务器通信中的响应速度。 但是对于 GQL，我们没有官方的解决方案。

结论

总之，虽然 GraphQL 与传统的 REST API 相比有很多好处，但它也有开发人员应该注意的缺点。 其中包括身份验证的挑战、变异堆管理的困难、文件处理的限制、各种错误处理方法以及缺乏标准化的缓存方法。 然而，通过仔细考虑和规划，开发人员可以克服这些挑战并利用 GraphQL 必须提供的许多优势。



原文:

GraphQL has quickly become a popular alternative to REST API for developers. It boasts a number of advantages, such as providing a more efficient way to retrieve data and enabling developers to declaratively specify what data they need. However, like any technology, GraphQL has its disadvantages that developers should be aware of. In this article, we will examine the top 5 GraphQL disadvantages and discuss how they can impact your development process.

Authentication

In GQL we have a schema in which we describe requests (queries, mutations, types). GQL is based on the assumption that this schema is static - not changing from the point of view of the application.

Is it different from REST? After all, in fact, there is no application in which API endpoints change. But not everything is like that. In the simplest example, you have app endpoints, some of which are available to an unauthorized user, and the rest, which are available to an authorized one, and these two parts do not intersect. Okay, obviously, all the API endpoints are available for an authenticated client. It will not work to simulate this on GQL. For example, you have a scheme in which most of the requests are not available to an unauthorized user - until he calls them, there is no way to know about it. That’s why it is not clear to us from the scheme how to use the API.

Mutations heap

GQL resembles an RPC (remote procedure call) - when we call some function and expect to get the result of execution

If Queries are built in such a way that it is clear to understand from what entity we could start writing a query and what actually we could get at the end, then there is a problem with Mutations. We open a GQL schema documentation and see a great heap of operations available to communicate with the service…

And I need to say more, mostly, the developers choose really bad naming for the Mutations and it’s really difficult to find all the operations connected to the same entity in the documentation, e.g. GitHub GQL API.

Files

As long as we exchange data via GQL, all is well. But sooner or later, you will need to download files. Yes, we have a multipart/form-data where we can attach a stream file to a request and let the server support it. My congratulations, this is a violation of one of the fundamental approaches of GQL - transport agnostic. GQL does not care what they work with: WebSockets, HTTP, carrier pigeons, etc.

GQL does not provide an answer to this question.

Error handling

The error could be one of two types:

  • Error - business logic error, and
  • Exception - which means you do some wrong operation with some wrong data, entity, etc.

In the case of exceptions, we have the concept of Top Level exception, when we are directly told: “The request failed for such and such a reason, because ...”.

With business logic Errors, the situation is different: you yourself must describe errors for each mutation for different cases. But in what format they will be, what information they will carry - it is not standardized.

Caching

When we used the CRUD, we had a list of available HTTP methods for our requests (GET, POST, etc.).

GQL denies all these patterns, it has not heard of idempotency, and it sends all requests using the POST HTTP method. The denial of data idempotency deprives us of very high accuracy - when we executed a request in a rough form, we could return a Cache header and indicate that we should cache some data for a while, so the browser put it in the cache and pick up ready-made data for us. Obviously, this speeded up responses from the server in client-server communication. But for GQL we don't have an official solution.

Conclusion

In conclusion, while GraphQL offers many benefits over traditional REST APIs, it also has its share of disadvantages that developers should be aware of. These include challenges with authentication, difficulty with mutation heap management, limitations with file handling, varied error handling approaches, and a lack of standardized caching methods. However, with careful consideration and planning, developers can overcome these challenges and leverage the many advantages that GraphQL has to offer.
